title: Review report — example detection pipeline
reviewer_independence: code-reviewer + compliance-reviewer, independent of the builder
tooling_coverage: ruff, mypy, bandit run; semgrep not installed (skipped, affected findings 🧠 inferred)
executive_summary: One warning and one style item. The threshold inconsistency should be reconciled with compliance before release; the missing docstring is non-blocking.
developer_guidance: Thresholds read cleanly but lack rationale comments (§4) - add the tuning date and the obligation each serves as you set them, so the next reviewer needn't re-derive intent.
limitations: Static review only (no execution consent); dynamic behaviour and true alert volumes are 🧠 inferred, not measured. semgrep was unavailable, so injection findings rest on manual inspection.

findings.title: Re-alert window inconsistent across two scenarios
findings.problem: Two scenarios read from the same config define different re-alert windows with no comment explaining why; a reader cannot tell if the difference is deliberate.
findings.likely_cause: Config drift - the second value was added later without reconciling the first.
findings.impact: A subject staying at elevated risk may not re-alert for the longer window, risking a missed status change.

fix.diff:
- REALERT_DAYS = 365
+ REALERT_DAYS = 60  # aligned with the sibling scenario; tuning date 2026-07-24, obligation …
fix.why: Aligns the two windows (or documents the deliberate difference), so the control is explainable to an auditor.

findings.title: Public scoring function missing a docstring
findings.problem: The public `score()` entry point has no docstring stating its inputs, outputs and assumptions.
findings.impact: Minor - slows the next developer; no correctness or detection impact.

fix.diff:
+ def score(order):
+     """Return the behavioural score for an order (0-100). Assumes prices are venue-normalised."""
fix.why: A one-line docstring makes the contract explicit without noise.
